name: Kymenlaakso (Finland)

colors.symbolism: the azure field of the regional arms, evoking the Kymi River and the surrounding waters
the silver lower field and the silver charge of the arms
the gold detail of the crown and accents

description: A square flag divided horizontally blue over white, charged centrally with the silver and gold arms of Kymenlaakso.
history: Kymenlaakso became a region under Finland's 1997 administrative reform; its banner is the heraldic flag of the regional coat of arms.

features.symbolism: the silver-and-gold composition of the regional arms, referring to the Kymi River valley and the historic frontier role of the region between Sweden and Russia.
